fix docs Docker build: install git for VitePress lastUpdated

VitePress needs git history to resolve lastUpdated timestamps.
Without git in the builder stage, the build fails.

Fix stale display when selected file is removed externally

When another program deletes the currently selected file, RTFM now
detects this in the main loop and forces a pane refresh within ~1
second. Previously the preview/image and top bar would show stale
data for the deleted file.

commit.message:
feat(tui): live filtering with `/` mode (#513)

## Summary

- `/` enters filter edit mode — real-time substring matching narrows
chat messages as you type
- Enter locks filter (applied mode), Esc clears — two-phase vim-style
lifecycle
- `!` prefix inverts filter (show non-matching messages)
- Match highlighting with background color on matched substrings in
rendered markdown
- Filter bar shows `/pattern█` with right-aligned match count (`3/47
matches`)
- Status bar shows active filter with `(Esc to clear)` hint in applied
mode
- `n`/`N` for next/prev match navigation in applied mode

## Files

- **New:** `state/filter.rs` — `FilterState`, `FilterScope` with cursor
editing, match navigation
- **New:** `update/filter.rs` — handlers for
open/close/input/backspace/clear/confirm/next/prev
- **New:** `view/filter_bar.rs` — filter bar rendering with blinking
cursor and match count
- **Modified:** `msg.rs` — 8 new `Filter*` message variants
- **Modified:** `mapping.rs` — `/` trigger, filter editing key routing,
applied mode interception
- **Modified:** `view/chat.rs` — message filtering and inline match
highlighting
- **Modified:** `view/mod.rs` — filter bar layout slot in chat area
- **Modified:** `view/status_bar.rs` — applied filter indicator replaces
old `active_filter` field
- **Modified:** `keybindings.rs` — Filter context in
`current_contexts()` and `context_label()`
- **Modified:** `app.rs` — `FilterState` replaces `active_filter:
Option<String>`

## Test plan

- [ ] Launch TUI, press `/` — filter bar appears at bottom of chat area
- [ ] Type `error` — messages without "error" disappear, matches
highlighted
- [ ] Press Enter — filter bar closes, filter stays active, status bar
shows `/error (Esc to clear)`
- [ ] Press `n`/`N` — cycles through matches
- [ ] Press Esc — filter clears, all messages return
- [ ] Press `/` then type `!error` — inverse: only messages WITHOUT
"error" shown
- [ ] Backspace on empty filter text closes filter mode
- [ ] Ctrl+U clears filter text but stays in edit mode
- [ ] `cargo check` and `cargo clippy` pass (no new warnings)

Co-authored-by: admin <admin@ardentleatherworks.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Launch readiness: security hardening, config cleanup, and performance indexes

- Add IP-based rate limiting to review helpful endpoint (1 vote/review/IP/hr)
- Block banned users from logging in and accessing /api/auth/me
- Require email verification for guest order lookups (prevents enumeration)
- Move CORS origins to env vars (ADMIN_URL), remove hardcoded production URLs
- Document all required env vars in .env.example (AUTH_SECRET, CLOUDINARY_*, etc.)
- Read shipping/points business values from SiteSetting with hardcoded fallbacks
- Add database indexes: Order[status,createdAt], Variant[isActive], StockMovement[createdAt]
- Return 429 for rate-limited analytics track requests (was silent 200)
- Fail gracefully if EMAIL_FROM env var is unset (was falling back to sandbox)
- Replace any types with proper Record<string, unknown> interfaces

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix whisper/broadcast messaging for instance (LFG) groups

UnitInParty/UnitInRaid only check the HOME party category, so the
OnlyPartyRaidGuild filter was silently blocking whispers to LFG group
members. Added UnitIsInMyGroup helper that iterates instance party/raid
tokens as a fallback, matching the pattern of IsInAnyGroup/IsInAnyRaid.

chore: rename npm package to @warrenbuckley/audio-visualizer

npm rejected the unscoped name 'audio-visualizer' as too similar to
the existing 'audiovisualizer' package and suggested the scoped name.

Updates all package name references across:
- package.json (name field)
- README.md (npm install + jsDelivr CDN URLs)
- CONTRIBUTING.md (imports, tarball filename, node_modules path, publish command → --access=public)
- src/index.ts (JSDoc example imports)
- docs: installation, quick-start, typescript, html, device-switching, index

Dist file names and the <audio-visualizer> tag name are unchanged.

commit.message:
Remove Recommendations tab; fix plural/phonetic similarity scoring

- Deletes the Recommendations tab entirely — tabs are now Overview and
  Category Details only
- Fixes scoring bug in similarity.ts: plural variant (SIM-004) and
  phonetic match (SIM-003) flags were firing correctly but the category
  score stayed at 0/CLEAR because the visual % alone was below every
  threshold. Now score is floored to 45 (MEDIUM) for plural matches and
  20 (LOW) for phonetic matches, ensuring the floor rule in assess.ts
  correctly elevates the application risk score

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix deep link timing: capture initial URL in Rust state before React mounts

The deep link URL arrives before React onOpenUrl listener registers.
Store the launch URL in Rust-side state and expose via get_initial_deep_link
command so the frontend can retrieve it on mount.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: onboarding location step resets to page 0 on Allow Location tap

AppRouter checked status==loading before onboardingDone, so calling
provider.initialize() during the location step swapped OnboardingScreen
out for a spinner, then rebuilt a fresh OnboardingScreen at page 0.

Fix: check onboardingDone first so the flow stays on OnboardingScreen
regardless of loading status during the onboarding steps.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
F1 2026 assets: refresh teams + schedule data

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat!: device-resident API for zero-copy GPU pipelines (v0.5.0)

Add device-resident methods that keep data on GPU between operations,
eliminating PCIe roundtrips for on-GPU pipelines like Merkle tree
construction:

- upload_keys/upload_values: H→D transfer to CudaSlice<u8>
- bulk_get_device: D→D lookup, returns (d_values, d_found)
- bulk_get_values_device: D→D values-only (no found mask, saves alloc)
- bulk_insert_device: D→D insert with 4-byte counter readback
- bulk_insert_device_uncounted: fully async D→D insert, no sync
- recount: GPU-side flag scan to correct len after uncounted inserts
- device(): expose Arc<CudaDevice> for shared context
- alloc_device/download: buffer management helpers

New CUDA kernel: flashmap_bulk_get_values_only (warp-cooperative,
same algorithm as bulk_get but without out_found output).

Re-exports CudaSlice and CudaDevice from cudarc for ergonomic use.
Adds GpuRequired error variant for device API on Rayon backend.
Consolidates COMPILE_QUERY into single block (INSERT first, QUERY second).

BREAKING: new public API surface, CudaSlice/CudaDevice re-exports.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: flash-map
repository.description: GPU-native concurrent hash map — 16B ops/sec on H100. Bulk-only API, SoA layout, CUDA kernels via cudarc.

commit.message:
Fix middleware: exclude API routes (they handle auth themselves)

Middleware was redirecting /api/chat and /api/services to /login,
which caused fetch() to silently follow the redirect and get HTML
back instead of the API response. API routes already have explicit
auth() checks returning 401, so middleware should only protect pages.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(frontend): 달력 뷰 개선 - 오늘 강조, 월별 요약, UI 스타일

- "오늘" 버튼으로 현재 월 복귀
- 오늘 날짜 테두리 강조 (cal-day.today)
- 날짜 클릭 가능 (clickable, selected 상태)
- 월별 감정 요약 (작성 일수, 상위 감정 칩)
- calMonthlySummary 컨테이너 추가

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(cmd/sim): R3 CLI validation gaps + dead code cleanup (#526)

* fix(cmd): add R3 validation for --num-requests, --prefix-tokens, --horizon (BC-1, BC-2, BC-3)

- --num-requests must be >= 0 (was silently accepted as negative)
- --prefix-tokens must be >= 0 (was silently accepted as negative)
- --horizon must be > 0 when explicitly set (was unchecked)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(sim): remove unused Bin struct from metrics_utils.go

Zero references found in codebase — dead code from an earlier iteration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(latency): remove 8 unused HFConfig accessor methods

GetString, GetFloat, GetInt, GetBool, GetStrings, MustGetString,
MustGetInt, Marshal — zero callers in codebase. GetModelConfig() uses
local closures on hf.Raw directly. Also removes unused errors import.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(cmd): unexport AllZeros → allZeros

Only used within cmd package. Go convention: unexport package-internal helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(cmd): move R3 validations before workload synthesis + add Changed() comment

- Move --num-requests and --prefix-tokens guards before the workload-type
  branching block so validation precedes first use (validate-before-use)
- Add inline comment explaining why --horizon uses Changed() guard
  (default is math.MaxInt64, unlike peer flags with positive defaults)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Fix location fetch loop and handle missing Google API key gracefully

  geocoding error

commit.message:
feat: BOM leaf → refInfo rawMaterialCode 기반 원재료 전개

BOM은 제품→부품(사출품/도장품) 1레벨만 존재하므로,
기준정보의 rawMaterialCode + netWeight/runnerWeight/cavity로
실제 원재료(PC수지, ABS수지 등) 소요량을 산출하도록 전환.

- 사출품: rawMaterialCode × (netWeight + runnerWeight/cavity) × lossRate → KG
- 도장품: rawMaterialCode × paintQty × lossRate / lotQty → KG
- 구매품: 부품 자체를 EA로 집계
- MRPView + StandardMaterialCostView 모두 적용

commit.message:
refactor: restructure project for true one-click deployment

🎯 Major Restructuring:

This is a fundamental redesign based on user feedback. The previous approach
was flawed: deploy with issues, then fix them. This is NOT one-click deployment.

The correct approach: deploy once with everything configured correctly.

📦 Changes:

1. Moved fix scripts to tools/ directory
   - fix-tailscale-routing.sh → tools/
   - fix-gateway-origin.sh → tools/
   - reset-gateway-token.sh → tools/
   - These are now maintenance tools, not deployment requirements

2. Added tools/README.md
   - Clearly explains these tools are for existing installations
   - New users don't need them
   - Deployment script handles everything correctly

3. Rewrote README.md
   - Emphasizes true one-click deployment
   - De-emphasizes fix scripts
   - Clear message: run deploy script, done!

4. Updated INDEX.txt
   - Reflects new project structure
   - Highlights one-click deployment
   - Maintenance tools clearly separated

5. Updated QUICK_REFERENCE.txt
   - Focus on deployment command
   - Maintenance tools marked as optional
   - Clear guidance for new users

6. Updated CHANGELOG.md
   - Documents this major restructuring
   - Explains design philosophy change
   - Shows before/after user experience

💡 Design Philosophy:

Before (WRONG):
  1. Run deployment script
  2. Discover configuration errors
  3. Run fix-tailscale-routing.sh
  4. Run fix-gateway-origin.sh
  5. Maybe run more fix scripts
  → This is "deploy + fix", not "one-click"

After (CORRECT):
  1. Run: bash openclaw-deploy.sh
  2. ✅ Done! Everything works immediately
  → This is TRUE one-click deployment

🎯 User Experience:

New users:
  - Run ONE command
  - Everything configured correctly from the start
  - No manual fixes needed
  - Immediate usability

Existing installations:
  - Maintenance tools available in tools/
  - Can fix manual configuration errors
  - Can reconfigure if needed

📊 Impact:

- Reduced deployment steps from 5+ to 1
- Eliminated confusion about fix scripts
- Clear separation: deployment vs maintenance
- Better first-time user experience

🙏 Credit:

This restructuring was prompted by excellent user feedback pointing out
the fundamental flaw in the original design. Thank you!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Wire lambd into ResidualFitter.fit() to activate empirical covariance blending

When self.lambd > 0, get_omega uses _get_omega_lambda which blends the
parametric covariance with the sample covariance:
  omega = (1-lambda) * omega_parametric + lambda * S_empirical

Previously lambd was stored but never used.

commit.message:
feat: add mailing list tool for fetching Ubuntu list archives

Fetches gzip'd mbox archives from lists.ubuntu.com, parses email
headers with mail-parser, filters to the last 30 days, threads
messages via In-Reply-To/References with subject fallback, and
deduplicates cross-posted threads. The prompt directs the agent to
always call mailing_list_threads when lists are configured, since
mailing list discussions are not tracked in Todoist.

commit.message:
fix: auto-sync Copilot org selector to current ticket's org on investigation open

When openInvestigation() loads a Jira ticket, the Copilot org dropdown was
staying on "All Clients" regardless of which client the ticket belonged to.
This caused the War Room Copilot to send null client_context to the API,
losing all org-specific connector/tool knowledge.

Fix: immediately after extracting customfield_10002 from the ticket, apply
the same namespace mapping logic used by runAIInvestigation() to find the
matching ORG_CONNECTORS entry and set copilotOrgSelect.value automatically.
The ticket's Jira org field (customfield_10002) is now the single source of
truth — no manual dropdown selection needed.

repository.name: noether
repository.description: This is our core repository, which contains the entire project structure, including contracts, scripts, and the UI.

commit.message:
Auto-provision Open WebUI external web search on startup (v0.5.29)

- Add scripts/provision-openwebui-search.sh for automatic configuration
- Configures external search URL: http://host.docker.internal:3080/api/openwebui/search
- Installs smart RAG template with knowledge priority guidance
- Installs aggressive query generation for time-sensitive queries
- Update start.sh to run provisioning after services start
- Update App.js documentation for auto-provisioned settings
- Only API key requires manual configuration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Remove subscription checks from TTS/STT — fixes 403 blocking all voice

Voice mode is already gated client-side (only paid users can enter) and
chat.js performs its own server-side subscription check. The redundant
subscription lookup in TTS/STT was failing for valid paid users, returning
403 and forcing the browser TTS fallback (robotic voice).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add port management script for OCI instance

- manage-ports.sh list: List all open ports
- manage-ports.sh add <port> <proto> <desc>: Add a port
- manage-ports.sh remove <port> <proto>: Remove a port

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: oci-terraform-instance
repository.description: Terraform configuration for OCI compute instance (A1 Flex)

commit.message:
feat(sitrep): throttle Telegram to every 10K steps

Dashboard sitrep still updates every 30min via cron. Telegram notifications
now only fire when current_step - last_telegram_step >= 10,000. Tracks
last_telegram_step in the history file so it persists across invocations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.description: We investigate whether a single Transformer can jointly learn autoregressive and masked diffusion objectives through shared weights, producing a dual-process language model inspired by Kahneman's System 1 / System 2 framework.
